verses.text: After seven months of plague the Philistines resolve to send the ark home, asking their priests how. Even pagan instinct knows you do not return what is holy 'empty.' Some sense of God's weight survives in every conscience; they dare not send Him back without an offering.

After seven months of plague the Philistines resolve to send the ark home, asking their priests how. Even pagan instinct knows you do not return what is holy 'empty.' Some sense of God's weight survives in every conscience; they dare not send Him back without an offering.

After seven months of plague the Philistines resolve to send the ark home, asking their priests how. Even pagan instinct knows you do not return what is holy 'empty.' Some sense of God's weight survives in every conscience; they dare not send Him back without an offering.

The priests prescribe a guilt offering of gold and warn, 'why harden your hearts, as the Egyptians and Pharaoh hardened theirs?' Even outsiders had learned the lesson of the Exodus. To resist God's hand, once it is plainly felt, is to invite the ruin that came on Egypt.

They devise a test: hitch two nursing cows, never yoked, to a cart, pen their calves, and watch. If the cows leave their calves and head straight for Israel, it is the LORD's doing, not chance. Honest seekers look for clear evidence of God's hand.

They devise a test: hitch two nursing cows, never yoked, to a cart, pen their calves, and watch. If the cows leave their calves and head straight for Israel, it is the LORD's doing, not chance. Honest seekers look for clear evidence of God's hand.

They devise a test: hitch two nursing cows, never yoked, to a cart, pen their calves, and watch. If the cows leave their calves and head straight for Israel, it is the LORD's doing, not chance. Honest seekers look for clear evidence of God's hand.

Against every instinct the cows go straight up the road to Beth-shemesh, lowing for their calves but never turning aside. Even brute beasts obey the LORD's unseen leading. What no nature could explain, God's hand plainly does, leaving the Philistines no doubt whose ark it was.

The men of Beth-shemesh, reaping wheat, look up and rejoice to see the ark returning. The Levites lift it down, and the very cart and cows become a burnt offering. Joy and worship are the right welcome when God's presence comes home to His people.

The five Philistine lords watch, then return to Ekron; the golden offerings stand as a witness in all their cities. The nations are made to confess, by their own gifts, the power of the God of Israel. Even enemies must one day own His hand.

The five Philistine lords watch, then return to Ekron; the golden offerings stand as a witness in all their cities. The nations are made to confess, by their own gifts, the power of the God of Israel. Even enemies must one day own His hand.

But some at Beth-shemesh peer into the ark, and God strikes them down. The same presence that blessed now warns: 'Who is able to stand before this holy LORD God?' Even returning joy must keep its reverence. The holy God is to be approached, never handled with careless curiosity.
